purpose: Two-model perplexity comparison (Hans et al. 2024). Scores a target against a scorer LLM + observer LLM pair; lower ratios indicate the target is more predictable under the scorer than the observer — a known signal for AI-generated text. v1 uses perplexity-ratio; v2 uses true cross-perplexity when tokenizers align.
use_when: You want a discrimination-evidence signal that's close to the published 95% AUC condition
You have access to a scorer + observer pair (default tinyllama + gpt2)
do_not_use_when: You need a binary verdict — ships uncalibrated by default
Target is in the scorer's or observer's training set (memorization biases the score)
You can't install the surprisal tier (transformers + torch, ~2 GB)

compute.cost_note: One-time ~600 MB tinyllama + ~500 MB gpt2 download; CPU inference acceptable

examples.description: Default scorer/observer pair (writes the evidence pack)
Consumer envelope on stdout (no evidence-pack side files)
